summary: The active Cascade Effects design system uses ink-lit Paper Architectures illustration, deterministic title policy, bold Inter-based title typography, and platform-specific safe-zone rules.

notes: The style-system JSON is the active token source for web and asset composition.
This contract governs package assets for YouTube, podcast, Web_CascadeEffects, Shorts covers, long-form living-cover visuals, chapter cards, and metadata images.
Ink-lit Paper Architectures is the canonical brand illustration direction; bright/daylight Paper Architectures and low-poly pastel launch references are legacy/inactive.
CascadeEffects.tv web episode-gallery thumbnails use the proof-v6 subject-only lane until a new approved subject-only proof replaces it.
Production exports remain outside normal git; approved reference renders may be tracked through Git LFS as source evidence.

sets.description: Canonical visual evidence for the active ink-lit Paper Architectures direction: deep ink-blue fields, cream paper forms, lavender shadows, cyan vellum glow, restrained coral accents, and clean model-lit paper construction.

policy: Ink-lit Paper Architectures reference renders are canonical visual evidence, not final production exports.
Approved web/channel identity reference renders may include the Cascade of Effects title when a package manifest explicitly allows a title-bearing source.
Episode thumbnails, Shorts covers, chapter cards, and package source art stay text-free unless their package manifest explicitly permits title-bearing source art.
Approved reference renders are tracked through Git LFS only under brand/assets/reference-renders/**.
The former bright/daylight Paper Architectures look and cascade-lowpoly-pastel-v1 references are legacy/inactive unless a package explicitly imports them as historical context.

tokens.rule: Use the active ink-lit paper palette with strong separation between deep ink fields, cream paper forms, lavender shadows, cyan vellum glow, and restrained coral warning accents. Do not revert to bright/daylight, low-poly plastic, dark documentary, noir, or evidence-board defaults unless a file is explicitly marked legacy.

typography.display: Inter 800 or equivalent bold blocky sans-serif.
typography.technical: IBM Plex Mono for small labels and metadata.
rules: Letter spacing must stay zero or positive.
The word of must be heavy enough to read at thumbnail and podcast-library sizes.
Do not rely on generated model text for final spelling.

titleTreatment.composition: Apply title locally after source artwork generation, except for approved web/channel identity reference renders explicitly allowed by a package manifest.
style: deep blue or lavender paper shadow/depth
high contrast against calm paper artwork zones
rules: No tagline on launch package assets.
No episode title unless a package manifest explicitly defines an episode-level template.
Profile icons must not carry the full title.
Episode thumbnails remain text-free unless a package manifest explicitly says otherwise.

safeZones: Every platform asset must declare safe zones in its package manifest.
Title-bearing assets must reserve a calm paper zone that stays readable at the smallest QA size.
CascadeEffects.tv episode-gallery thumbnails must use subject-only crops with no title inside the image frame, no evidence/system clutter, and no decorative cyan/coral artifacts.
layout.density: Prefer one dominant folded-paper public anchor or folded cascade model, ink-lit negative space, one or two terraces/cascade sheets, and only a few supporting artifacts when the format can sustain them.

webEpisodeGallery.rule: One dominant primary subject only; the card pill/title carries case context.

layout.textureNoise: Generated source-art proofs, package candidates, thumbnails, and motion-readiness candidates must record texture_noise_read: pass|tighten|reject. Use subtle paper fiber only and clean low-detail paper planes; no speckle/noise/grain/grit/sandy texture.
layout.responsiveness: Web and social assets must derive from platform-native compositions, not one crop reused everywhere.

colorPolicy.dominantPalette: Deep ink-blue fields, warm cream paper, powder cyan vellum glow, muted lavender shadows, soft graphite seams, and restrained coral warning accents.
accentUse: Use coral sparingly for warning or failure cues only.
Use cyan for dry vellum cascade sheets and signal emphasis only where causality needs to be shown.
Use lavender for paper shadows and depth on dark fields.
Use cream paper forms against deep ink fields so assets stay readable at small sizes.
colorPolicy.legacyBan: The prior bright/daylight Paper Architectures, low-poly plastic, dark evidence-board, neon signal green, and archival/evidence collage grammars are inactive defaults.

compositionPolicy.sourceArtwork: Generated no-text ink-lit Paper Architectures artwork first, except approved title-bearing web/channel identity source references named by a package manifest.
compositionPolicy.webEpisodeGalleryThumbnails: For CascadeEffects.tv episode-gallery cards, keep source art subject-only and text-free. Start future prompts with the primary subject and ban separate evidence trays, ledgers, gauges, connection details, system diagrams, arrows, dry signal paths, secondary prop clusters, and decorative cyan/coral artifacts.
compositionPolicy.sceneCompositing: After a baseline Paper Architectures illustration is approved, small additions or placement changes must be handled as deterministic layer composites over the locked baseline, not by regenerating the full scene.
compositionPolicy.driftControl: Composited layers must record source paths, placement, scale, rotation, opacity, and output path so approved paper material language, minimal texture-noise level, palette, and object density do not drift between iterations. High-frequency speckle, visible grain, grit, sandy/noisy paper surfaces, texture-heavy rendering, or texture that competes with the public anchor requires texture_noise_read: tighten|reject and blocks promotion.
compositionPolicy.qaPreview: Produce contact sheets or platform mockups for profile crop, podcast-library square, YouTube thumbnail sizes, banner safe-zone crop, Shorts phone scale, and web social cards.
compositionPolicy.sourceControl: Track contracts, manifests, validators, templates, and provenance. Do not track generated raster media.

rules: Do not use cascade-paper-architectures-v1 bright/daylight language as active package guidance for new assets.
Do not use cascade-lowpoly-pastel-v1 as active package guidance for new assets.
Do not use legacy dark visual language, noir, gritty documentary, or evidence-board defaults as active package guidance.
Do not let older token files, screenshots, or generated images override this ink-lit contract.

qa.description: Every title-bearing asset spells Cascade of Effects exactly.
Title-bearing assets remain readable at platform-library preview sizes.
Contracts and manifests pass the asset-package schema and custom validator.
Every generated source-art proof, package candidate, thumbnail, or motion-readiness candidate records texture_noise_read: pass|tighten|reject; only pass may advance.
Live CascadeEffects.tv episode-gallery thumbnails point at the active proof-v6 subject-only folder, avoid rejected/stale proof folders, and keep alt text free of water/evidence-clutter language.
